title: Grandpa's Story
story_html:
Emma's teacher gave the class a project: interview a family member about an important topic from their life. Emma chose her grandfather right away.

That week was busy with soccer practice, so Emma had little time to prepare. Every evening, after she finished her last stretch on the field, she would review her list of questions instead of resting.

On Saturday, Emma's older brother offered to direct her to Grandpa's apartment building, since she had never gone there alone. She pulled her notebook out of her bag on the walk over, wanting to make the visit as good as possible.

Grandpa opened the door wearing his old wool cap, his gentle voice making Emma feel calm right away. Sitting at his cluttered wooden desk, he began to share stories about his own younger days. He said he had once helped build the tall clock tower in the center of town.

Emma had to concentrate hard to catch every word. It made sense that Grandpa had so many stories, since he had traveled and worked in so many places. In case she forgot a question, she kept a spare pen ready, though she felt certain she wouldn't need it.

She noticed the skin on his hands was rough and lined from years of hard work. The room grew a little dark as the sun went down, so Grandpa turned on a small lamp. He showed her an old photograph, and at first Emma did not recognize the young man smiling in it — it was Grandpa, many years ago.

Emma felt responsible for getting every detail right, and she understood her grandfather's story on a whole new level by the time they finished. Her notebook, once empty, was now filled with page after page of notes, each one holding more value than she had expected.

When they were done, Grandpa gently touched Emma's shoulder and smiled, proud of the young reporter he had raised.

busy.def: Having a lot of things to do.

stretch.def: To make your body or a part of it longer or straighter.

direct.def: To show someone the way to go.

possible.def: Able to happen or be done.

review.def: To look at something again carefully to check it.

wear.def: To have clothes on your body.

topic.def: A subject that people talk or write about.

sense.def: A feeling or awareness of something.

case.def: A situation, or a container for holding things.

gentle.def: Kind, soft, and careful, not rough.

skin.def: The outer covering of a person's body.

recognize.def: To know someone or something because you have seen it before.

responsible.def: Being the person who should take care of something.

desk.def: A table used for writing or studying.

share.def: To use or have something together with others.

value.def: How much something is worth, or its importance.

cap.def: A soft hat, often with a curved part at the front.

level.def: A stage or standard that something has reached.

interview.def: To ask someone questions to learn more about them.

concentrate.def: To give all your attention to something.

touch.def: To put your hand on something.

questions.qtext: In "Emma pulled her notebook out of her bag on the walk over, wanting to make the visit as good as possible," what does possible mean?
options: able to happen or be done
questions.explain: B — Emma wanted the visit to be the best it could possibly be.

questions.qtext: In "Emma had to concentrate hard to catch every word," what does concentrate mean?
options: to give all your attention to something
to speak in a loud voice
questions.explain: A — Emma focused hard so she would not miss anything Grandpa said.

questions.qtext: In "Emma did not recognize the young man smiling in it — it was Grandpa, many years ago," what does recognize mean?
options: to draw a picture of someone
to forget someone's name on purpose
to know someone because you have seen them before
questions.explain: C — Emma could not tell the young man in the old photo was Grandpa because he looked so different.

questions.qtext: Why did Emma review her questions every evening even though her week was busy?
options: Her teacher checked her notebook every day
She wanted to be well prepared for the interview with Grandpa
She had nothing else to do after soccer practice
Her brother told her she had to
questions.explain: B — Even with a busy schedule, Emma made time to review her questions to prepare well for the interview.

questions.qtext: How did the interview change what was in Emma's notebook?
options: It stayed empty because Grandpa did not want to talk
It went from empty to full of notes worth a lot to her
She lost her notebook at Grandpa's apartment
She used it to draw pictures instead of taking notes
questions.explain: B — Her once-empty notebook was filled with pages of notes that she came to value greatly.
